sections.title: Leadership
sections.summary: Robert A. Bradway serves as Chairman of the Board, Chief Executive Officer, and President, giving one named leader both executive control and board leadership. Under this leadership, Amgen reported $28.19 billion of FY2025 revenue, $6.717 billion of net income, and continued to expand approved medicines and late-stage programs.
points: Bradway holds the combined Chairman, CEO, and President roles, so investors should weigh clear accountability against the reduced separation between board leadership and management.
The 2026 proxy highlights a broad operating year: 18 products achieved record sales, 14 products exceeded $1 billion in sales, and 13 products delivered double-digit sales growth.

Amgen advanced six global Phase 3 studies for MariTide across chronic weight management, cardiovascular outcomes, heart failure, and obstructive sleep apnea, showing management is funding large late-stage programs.

sections.title: Capital Allocation
sections.summary: Amgen generated $8.471 billion of FY2025 operating cash flow and ended the year with $11.973 billion of cash and equivalents, giving management meaningful funding capacity. The balance sheet also carried $56.549 billion of long-term debt and $3.661 billion of stockholders' equity, so capital allocation discipline matters.
points: Amgen announced $900 million of additional investment in Ohio and $650 million in Puerto Rico in 2025, following a $1 billion North Carolina expansion announced in 2024.

The manufacturing investments are intended to support increased drug production and integrate advanced process development technologies that improve yield, resilience, and flexibility across the supply network.
Amgen made the final $1.8 billion U.S. repatriation tax payment in 2025 under the 2017 Tax Act installment election, removing that disclosed installment obligation.

sections.title: Shareholder Alignment
sections.summary: The packet supports some shareholder-alignment practices, including an advisory vote on executive compensation and governance outreach with holders of approximately 59% of outstanding shares. However, no detailed compensation structure, insider ownership, or capital-return policy is provided, limiting the evidence for a stronger grade.
points: The 2026 annual meeting agenda includes an advisory vote on executive compensation, giving shareholders a recurring channel to express approval or dissatisfaction with pay outcomes.
Amgen said it conducted governance-focused outreach with stockholders holding approximately 59% of outstanding shares since the prior annual meeting, a concrete engagement effort.
The proxy's operating scorecard emphasizes patient reach, product sales records, FDA approvals, and Phase 3 progress, but the packet does not provide detailed executive-pay metrics.

sections.title: Communication
sections.summary: Amgen's investor disclosures include SEC company facts for revenue, net income, operating cash flow, cash, debt, and equity, plus proxy detail on product sales milestones and pipeline progress. The 10-K also identifies specific patent, biosimilar, and antitrust litigation matters, giving investors named issues to track.
points: The SEC company facts summary reports FY2025 revenue of $28.19 billion, net income of $6.717 billion, and operating cash flow of $8.471 billion.
The 2026 proxy discloses product-level milestones, including FDA approvals for IMDELLTRA, LUMAKRAS with Vectibix, UPLIZNA, and TEZSPIRE in specific indications.
The 10-K names patent and biosimilar litigation involving Prolia/XGEVA, PAVBLU, KYPROLIS, and TAVNEOS, including injunction and damages requests in federal courts.

sections.title: Governance
sections.summary: Amgen has annual director elections on the 2026 agenda, an auditor ratification vote for Ernst & Young LLP, and an advisory compensation vote. Governance is tempered by the combined Chairman and CEO role, the board's opposition to an independent-chair proposal, and disclosed litigation and antitrust risks.
points: The 2026 annual meeting agenda includes election of 12 directors for terms expiring at the 2027 annual meeting, keeping board seats subject to annual shareholder review.
The board recommended voting against a stockholder proposal requiring an independent board chairman, while Robert A. Bradway remains Chairman, CEO, and President.
Amgen disclosed a Delaware jury verdict against it in the Regeneron antitrust case, while stating it had not recorded a liability because loss was not considered probable.

types.type: Diversified biologic and biosimilar medicine portfolio
types.summary: Amgen's portfolio has breadth beyond a single flagship product: in 2025, 18 products achieved record sales, 14 products exceeded $1 billion in sales, and 13 products delivered double-digit sales growth. The company describes its innovative and biosimilar medicine portfolios as serving millions of patients globally.

Amgen reported several FDA approvals, including IMDELLTRA for second-line or later extensive-stage small cell lung cancer and LUMAKRAS with Vectibix for third-line KRAS G12C-mutated metastatic colorectal cancer. It also initiated and advanced six global Phase 3 MariTide studies across four obesity and obesity-related conditions.

types.type: Biologics manufacturing scale and supply investment
types.summary: Amgen is adding manufacturing capacity through $900 million of additional Ohio investment and $650 million in Puerto Rico in 2025, following a $1 billion North Carolina expansion announced in 2024. The stated goal is higher drug production, advanced process development technologies, improved yield, resilience, and supply-network flexibility.

Amgen operates at large scale in the SEC SIC category "Biological Products, (No Diagnostic Substances)," with FY2025 revenue of $28.19 billion and operating cash flow of $8.471 billion. That scale supports funding for manufacturing expansions, late-stage Phase 3 trials, and multiple approved medicine franchises.
